name: Orchestrator role + operational-rule codification (NDEBT-010, -013, -014, -019, -020)

design_notes.what_this_feature_is: Phase-006 F-053: define the load-bearing-but-undefined Orchestrator role and codify four incident-proven operational rules. (NDEBT-010) standard/AGF.md 0.2.0 Section 2 defines the Orchestrator -- coordination/state-ownership: sequences the four execution roles, parses each gate's JSON verdict and advances only on a pass, owns run_state's run-position/coordination fields (Section 5 rule 4), enforces the circuit breaker. The references in capability_profiles.md (orchestrator-primary + 'every role maps' -> a true 5-role/5-profile correspondence), permission_classes.md, mcp_policy.md, and methodology/06_release_train.md 0.3.1 (concession retired) all now resolve; three stale 'four agent roles' AGF descriptions fixed in-scope. (NDEBT-013/020) methodology/02 0.4.0 Section 11 codifies probe isolation. (NDEBT-019) Section 10 anti-pattern (e) codifies explicit exit-code assertion + the set -e guard. (NDEBT-014) Section 12 catalogues the five verification-authoring defect classes; the mechanizable one is added as tools/verify_lib.sh's sixth primitive vlib_word_present (whole-word match), fixture-tested.
design_notes.foundational_standards_change: This is a DELIBERATE standards change to a foundational document (standard/AGF.md's role model: 'The Four Agent Roles' -> a coordinating Orchestrator plus four execution roles), exactly as NDEBT-010's own remediation prescribed ('a deliberate standards change to route through the next planned phase, not a hot-patch'). The change is additive and reconciling -- it defines a role every other governance file already referenced -- and does not alter the four execution roles, the dual validator gate, the verdict parse rule, or the durable-state rule.
design_notes.next_feature_decision: The feature-choice AskUserQuestion aborted (permission stream closed, as it did before feature 050). Proceeded with the RECOMMENDED option (053) under the phase-006 activation's standing 'Approved. Proceed with the logical next steps.' authorization and the operator's explicit 'Continue from where you left off.', disclosing the abort-driven choice here + in the PR so the operator may redirect at review (the PR is the steer mechanism; nothing merges without the operator). F-050 precedent for an aborted-question -> proceed-with-disclosure.
design_notes.dogfooding_note: E10 deliberately PRACTICES the NDEBT-019 rc-capture rule this feature codifies -- it captures validate.sh's own exit code explicitly (out=$(cmd); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0) rather than relying on a piped SUMMARY grep, so the feature's own evidence exemplifies the standard it adds.
design_notes.process_honesty: Single-session collapsed-role execution (046-052 precedent) -- now itself disclosed-and-permitted by the new AGF Orchestrator clause ('Where a deployment collapses the Orchestrator into another role, that collapse MUST be disclosed in durable state and every gate below still honoured'). The load-bearing probe (E09) uses a minimal scratch copy under a session-scratch root, never mutating the real tree -- practicing the Section 11 Probe Isolation rule this feature adds. NDEBT-014's two non-mechanized classes (HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity, two-part .agent immutability) are documented as authoring patterns in Section 12 rather than standalone primitives, because they are contextual, not pure functions -- stated plainly, not hidden.

files_create.purpose: Positive fixture for vlib_word_present: contains the whole word 'new' as a delimited token.
Negative fixture for vlib_word_present: 'new' appears only inside 'renewed', so a whole-word probe must reject it.

files_modify.purpose: 0.2.0: define the Orchestrator in Section 2 (role-table row + coordination/state-ownership language; heading generalised from 'The Four Agent Roles'); Section 5 rule 4 names the Orchestrator-owned run_state coordination fields (NDEBT-010).
0.3.1: Section 6 release-mechanics ownership no longer concedes 'AGF defines no separate release-manager role' -- assigned to the now-defined Orchestrator (NDEBT-010 reconciliation).
0.2.1: the AGF module description no longer says 'the four agent roles' (names the coordinating Orchestrator plus four execution roles).
0.1.1: Section 7 reference to AGF updated from 'the four agent roles' to name the Orchestrator + four execution roles.
0.2.1: Section 7 reference to AGF updated likewise.
0.4.0: Section 10 anti-pattern (e) exit-code assertion (NDEBT-019); Section 11 Probe Isolation (NDEBT-013/020); Section 12 the five-class verification-authoring defect catalogue (NDEBT-014).

Add the sixth vetted primitive vlib_word_present (whole-word/delimited-token match, fixing the substring-on-containing-word false-pass); header primitive count 5 -> 6 (NDEBT-014 class 4 mechanization).
Add the two vlib_word_present fixtures to the manifest (completeness guard now accounts for 43 fixtures).
[Unreleased] Added: the Orchestrator role + the methodology/02 codifications + vlib_word_present.

non_goals: No content change to capability_profiles.md, permission_classes.md, or mcp_policy.md -- they already reference/allocate the Orchestrator; defining it in AGF makes their references resolve (the 5-profile <-> 5-role correspondence becomes true without editing them).

No new standalone verify_lib.sh primitive for NDEBT-014 classes 1-2 (HEAD-anchored anti-vacuity, two-part .agent immutability) -- they are documented authoring patterns in Section 12, not pure functions ('where mechanizable' per NDEBT-014's remediation); only the whole-word matcher is cleanly mechanizable.

No change to schema/run_state.schema.json (the field-ownership is a document-binding AGF rule, not a schema constraint) and no change to the four execution-role definitions, the dual validator gate, the verdict parse rule, or the durable-state rule.

No edit to any .agent/evidence/* historical capture (the NDEBT-019 codification binds future contracts; past captures stay immutable per AH-3), and no new shipped fixtures beyond the two vlib_word_present ones.

verification.acceptance_test: ANTI-VACUITY (HEAD~1 = c2fabe3, the F-052 merge / PR #31 squash): at HEAD~1 AGF Section 2 was 'The Four Agent Roles' with NO Orchestrator row, methodology/02 had NO Section 11, and verify_lib had NO vlib_word_present.
verification.command:
set -e
git show HEAD~1:standard/AGF.md | grep -Fq 'The Four Agent Roles'
git show HEAD~1:standard/AGF.md | grep -Fq '| **Orchestrator** |' && { echo 'VACUITY: Orchestrator row already present'; exit 1; } || true
git show HEAD~1:methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md | grep -Fq '## 11. Probe Isolation' && { echo 'VACUITY: Section 11 already present'; exit 1; } || true
git show HEAD~1:tools/verify_lib.sh | grep -Fq 'vlib_word_present' && { echo 'VACUITY: primitive already present'; exit 1; } || true
echo 'E01 PASS: at HEAD~1 no Orchestrator role, no Section 11, no vlib_word_present (anti-vacuity)'

verification.acceptance_test: ORCHESTRATOR DEFINED (NDEBT-010, acceptance test 1): AGF Section 2 defines the Orchestrator as a role-table row, and Section 5 rule 4 names its exclusively-owned run_state coordination fields.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq '## 2. The Agent Roles' standard/AGF.md
grep -Fq '| **Orchestrator** |' standard/AGF.md
grep -Fq 'owned exclusively by the **Orchestrator**' standard/AGF.md
echo 'E02 PASS: AGF Section 2 defines the Orchestrator (role row + Section 5 rule 4 field ownership)'

verification.acceptance_test: CROSS-REFERENCES RESOLVE (NDEBT-010, acceptance test 1): the load-bearing references resolve to the AGF Orchestrator, the 06_release_train concession is retired, and AGF now has 5 role rows matching capability_profiles' 5 profiles.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'orchestrator-primary' standard/capability_profiles.md
grep -Fq '| Orchestrator |' standard/permission_classes.md
grep -Fq 'Orchestrator, Planner, Validator' standard/mcp_policy.md
grep -Fq 'belong to the **Orchestrator**' methodology/06_release_train.md
grep -Fq 'these mechanics sit with the Orchestrator' methodology/06_release_train.md
roles=$(grep -c '^| \*\*' standard/AGF.md); test "$roles" -eq 5
grep -Fq 'The Five Standard Profiles' standard/capability_profiles.md
echo "E03 PASS: cross-refs resolve; AGF role rows=$roles <-> 5 capability profiles; release-train mechanics now assigned to the defined Orchestrator (positive assertion, no negated grep)"

verification.acceptance_test: NO STALE 'four agent roles' AGF DESCRIPTIONS: the three cross-references that described AGF as 'the four agent roles' now name the coordinating Orchestrator (enumeration-completeness follow-through).
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'a coordinating Orchestrator plus four execution roles' standard/README.md
grep -Fq 'the coordinating Orchestrator and the' methodology/01_execution.md
grep -Fq 'coordinating Orchestrator and the' methodology/00_planning.md
echo 'E04 PASS: the 3 four-agent-roles AGF cross-refs now name the coordinating Orchestrator'

verification.acceptance_test: PROBE ISOLATION CODIFIED (NDEBT-013/020, acceptance test 2): methodology/02 Section 11 binds the scratch-paths-only, cwd-before-destructive, and restore-with-trap rules and cites the incidents.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq '## 11. Probe Isolation' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'Scratch paths only' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'Verify the working directory before any destructive command' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'back it up and restore it with a trap-based' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'NDEBT-013' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'NDEBT-020' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
echo 'E05 PASS: Section 11 codifies probe isolation (scratch-only rule 1, cwd-before-destructive rule 2, restore-with-trap rule 3) citing NDEBT-013/020'

verification.acceptance_test: RC-CAPTURE CODIFIED (NDEBT-019, acceptance test 2): methodology/02 Section 10 anti-pattern (e) requires explicit exit-code capture and the set -e guard for expected-FAIL captures; the summary rule now reads 'all five'.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'Exit codes swallowed by a pipeline' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'set +e; out=$(cmd); rc=$?; set -e' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'NDEBT-019' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'all five' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
echo 'E06 PASS: Section 10 (e) requires explicit rc capture + set -e guard (NDEBT-019); summary now all five'

verification.acceptance_test: FIVE-CLASS CATALOGUE CODIFIED (NDEBT-014, acceptance test 2): methodology/02 Section 12 catalogues all five verification-authoring defect classes with fixes and names vlib_word_present.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq '## 12. The Verification-Authoring Defect Catalogue' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'NDEBT-014' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
grep -Fq 'vlib_word_present' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
n=$(awk '/## 12\. The Verification-Authoring Defect Catalogue/{s=1} s&&/^[0-9]\. \*\*/{c++} END{print c+0}' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md)
test "$n" -eq 5
echo "E07 PASS: Section 12 catalogues all $n classes (NDEBT-014) naming vlib_word_present"

verification.acceptance_test: vlib_word_present WORKS + IS FIXTURE-TESTED (acceptance test 3): the primitive matches a whole word and rejects a containing-word substring, and both fixtures are exercised by tools/fixtures_self_test.sh.
verification.command:
set -e
source tools/verify_lib.sh
vlib_word_present tools/fixtures/word_present_pass.md new
set +e; vlib_word_present tools/fixtures/word_present_fail.md new; rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -ne 0
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1)
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +primitive +vlib_word_present pass'
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'OK +primitive +vlib_word_present fail'
echo 'E08 PASS: vlib_word_present matches whole-word, rejects containing-word; both fixtures exercised by the self-test'

verification.acceptance_test: vlib_word_present FIXTURE IS LOAD-BEARING: corrupting word_present_fail.md to contain a whole-word 'new' makes the self-test FAIL. Probed in a minimal scratch copy (Section 11 probe isolation).
verification.command:
set -e
S="$SB/e09"; rm -rf "$S"; mkdir -p "$S"; cp -a tools schema NIZAM.json "$S/"
printf 'This fixture now contains a new standalone token.\n' > "$S/tools/fixtures/word_present_fail.md"
set +e; out=$(bash "$S/tools/fixtures_self_test.sh" 2>&1); rc=$?; set -e
test $rc -ne 0
printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep -Eq 'FAIL .*vlib_word_present fail'
rm -rf "$S"
echo 'E09 PASS: corrupting word_present_fail to hold a whole-word new makes the self-test FAIL (fixture load-bearing)'

verification.acceptance_test: SELF-TEST 43/43 + REGRESSION BUNDLE (dogfoods NDEBT-019 rc-capture): the two new fixtures are accounted for; default 13/13, payload 11/11 with EXPLICIT exit-code capture, e2e green.
verification.command:
set -e
out=$(bash tools/fixtures_self_test.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Eq 'SELF-TEST OK: 43/43 fixtures accounted for, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY: 13 passed, 0 failed'
out=$(bash tools/validate.sh --payload 2>&1); rc=$?; test $rc -eq 0; printf '%s\n' "$out" | tail -1 | grep -Fxq 'SUMMARY (payload mode): 11 passed, 0 failed'
bash tools/e2e_bootstrap_test.sh >/dev/null 2>&1
echo 'E10 PASS: self-test 43/43; default 13/13 (rc=0), payload 11/11 (rc=0), e2e green'

verification.acceptance_test: DELIVERABLE CONFINEMENT: HEAD~1..HEAD touches exactly the twelve contracted files and no .agent path.
verification.command:
set -e
test "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD | LC_ALL=C sort | tr '\n' ' ')" = 'CHANGELOG.md docs/planning/DEBT.md methodology/00_planning.md methodology/01_execution.md methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md methodology/06_release_train.md standard/AGF.md standard/README.md tools/fixtures/word_present_fail.md tools/fixtures/word_present_pass.md tools/fixtures_self_test.sh tools/verify_lib.sh '
test -z "$(git diff --name-only HEAD~1..HEAD -- .agent/)"
echo 'E11 PASS: confined to the 12 contracted files, zero .agent'

verification.acceptance_test: DOCS/DEBT SYNCED: DEBT 0.23.0 has NDEBT-010/013/014/019/020 in Resolved; AGF 0.2.0; methodology/02 0.4.0; CHANGELOG names the Orchestrator role entry.
verification.command:
set -e
grep -Fq 'version: 0.23.0' docs/planning/DEBT.md
python3 - <<'PY'
import re
sect=None; resolved=set()
for l in open('docs/planning/DEBT.md'):
    if l.startswith('## Open'): sect='open'
    elif l.startswith('## Resolved'): sect='resolved'
    m=re.match(r'\| (NDEBT-\d+) ', l)
    if m and sect=='resolved': resolved.add(m.group(1))
    if l.startswith(('| NDEBT-010 ','| NDEBT-013 ','| NDEBT-014 ','| NDEBT-019 ','| NDEBT-020 ')):
        assert sect=='resolved', l[:14]+' not Resolved'
for n in ('NDEBT-010','NDEBT-013','NDEBT-014','NDEBT-019','NDEBT-020'): assert n in resolved, n
print('debt ok')
PY
grep -Fq 'version: 0.2.0' standard/AGF.md
grep -Fq 'version: 0.4.0' methodology/02_adversarial_tdd.md
sed -n '/## \[Unreleased\]/,/## \[0.7.0\]/p' CHANGELOG.md | grep -Fq 'Orchestrator role + operational-rule codification'
echo 'E12 PASS: DEBT 0.23.0 (5 NDEBTs Resolved); AGF 0.2.0; methodology/02 0.4.0; CHANGELOG synced'

evidence_convention: Each verification command's captured output is in .agent/evidence/053-verify-NN.txt (12 entries), plus the consolidated .agent/evidence/053/verification.txt (12 EXIT:0 markers) and .agent/evidence/053/diff.patch. Commands run from the repo root at deliverable commit b848aac (HEAD), with HEAD~1 = the F-052 merge c2fabe3 as the anti-vacuity anchor; the load-bearing probe (E09) builds a MINIMAL scratch copy (tools/+schema/+NIZAM.json) under a session-scratch root $SB, never mutating the real tree (Section 11 probe isolation).

approvals.process_note: Operator-directed phase execution (ungated DAG root). The feature-choice AskUserQuestion aborted; proceeded with the recommended 053 under standing authorization + the operator's 'Continue', disclosed here + in the PR for redirect-at-review (F-050 precedent). No separate Loop-1 gate agents; the 12-entry suite executed live with captured evidence (dogfooding the NDEBT-019 rc-capture rule it adds); QA record .agent/qa/053.json documents the independent re-run. Scope UNDER estimate (221 vs 260); a foundational AGF standards change, additive and reconciling.
